# Audit Log

% !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
% !! This file is generated by oca-gen-addon-readme !!
% !! changes will be overwritten.                   !!
% !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
% !! source digest: sha256:a90e698ee182e3347a4281f0af8c17b456563c81e7f65d8317600206d87b5ee1
% !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

{{ badge1 }} {{ badge2 }} {{ badge3 }} {{ badge4 }} {{ badge5 }}

This module allows the administrator to log user operations performed on data
models such as `create`, `read`, `write` and `delete`.

**Table of contents**

```{contents}
:local: true
```

## Configuration

### Rules

Go to `Settings / Technical / Audit / Rules` to subscribe rules. A rule defines
which operations to log for a given data model.

```{image} https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OCA/server-tools/12.0/auditlog/static/description/rule.png
```

### Cleanup

A scheduled action exists to delete logs older than 6 months (180 days)
automatically but is not enabled by default.
To activate it and/or change the delay, go to the
`Configuration / Technical / Automation / Scheduled Actions` menu and edit the
`Auto-vacuum audit logs` entry:

```{image} https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OCA/server-tools/12.0/auditlog/static/description/autovacuum.png
```

In case you're having trouble with the amount of records to delete per run,
you can pass the amount of records to delete for one model per run as the second
parameter, the default is to delete all records in one go.

### Security / Policy

You need to be aware that logging can contain sensitive data. With raw HTTP logs,
there not much to do about that, but for ORM logging, the module blacklists all
fields called `password`. If you really need to audit that (having plain passwords
laying around is a policy violation basically everywhere), rename your field or
create a related field used for input.

## Usage

Check logs in the `Settings / Technical / Audit / Logs` menu. You can
group them by user sessions, date, data model or HTTP requests:

```{image} https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OCA/server-tools/12.0/auditlog/static/description/logs.png
```

Get the details:

```{image} https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OCA/server-tools/12.0/auditlog/static/description/log.png
```

## Known issues / Roadmap

- Log only operations triggered by some users. Currently it logs all users.
- Logging read operations does not work on all data models. Need investigation.

## Bug Tracker

Bugs are tracked on [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/OCA/server-tools/issues).
In case of trouble, please check there if your issue has already been reported.
If you spotted it first, help us to smash it by providing a detailed and welcomed
[feedback](https://github.com/OCA/server-tools/issues/new?body=module:%20auditlog%0Aversion:%2012.0%0A%0A**Steps%20to%20reproduce**%0A-%20...%0A%0A**Current%20behavior**%0A%0A**Expected%20behavior**).

Do not contact contributors directly about support or help with technical issues.

modules.name: odoo.addons.auditlog.models.http_request
documentation: Copyright 2015 ABF OSIELL <https://osiell.com>
License AGPL-3.0 or later (https://www.gnu.org/licenses/agpl).

methods.name: odoo.addons.auditlog.models.http_request.AuditlogHTTPRequest.current_http_request
documentation:
Create a log corresponding to the current HTTP request, and returns
its ID. This method can be called several times during the
HTTP query/response cycle, it will only log the request on the
first call.
If no HTTP request is available, returns `False`.

modules.name: odoo.addons.auditlog.models.http_session
documentation: Copyright 2015 ABF OSIELL <https://osiell.com>
License AGPL-3.0 or later (https://www.gnu.org/licenses/agpl).

methods.name: odoo.addons.auditlog.models.http_session.AuditlogtHTTPSession.current_http_session
documentation:
Create a log corresponding to the current HTTP user session, and
returns its ID. This method can be called several times during the
HTTP query/response cycle, it will only log the user session on the
first call.
If no HTTP user session is available, returns `False`.

methods.name: odoo.addons.auditlog.models.autovacuum.AuditlogAutovacuum.autovacuum
documentation:
Delete all logs older than `days`. This includes:
: - CRUD logs (create, read, write, unlink)
  - HTTP requests
  - HTTP user sessions

Called from a cron.

methods.name: odoo.addons.auditlog.tests.test_auditlog.AuditlogCommon.test_LogCreation
documentation: First test, caching some data.

methods.name: odoo.addons.auditlog.tests.test_auditlog.AuditlogCommon.test_LogCreation2
documentation: Second test, using cached data of the first one.

methods.name: odoo.addons.auditlog.tests.test_auditlog.AuditlogCommon.test_LogCreation3
documentation:
Third test, two groups, the latter being the parent of the former.
Then we remove it right after (with (2, X) tuple) to test the creation
of a 'write' log with a deleted resource (so with no text
representation).

methods.name: odoo.addons.auditlog.tests.test_auditlog.AuditlogCommon.test_LogCreation4
documentation:
Fourth test, create several records at once (with create multi
feature starting from Odoo 12) and check that the same number of logs
has been generated.

methods.name: odoo.addons.auditlog.tests.test_auditlog.AuditlogCommon.test_LogCreation5
documentation:
Fifth test, create a record and check that the same number of logs
has been generated. And then delete it, check that it has created log
with 0 fields updated.

methods.name: odoo.addons.auditlog.tests.test_auditlog.AuditlogCommon.test_LogCreation6
documentation:
Six test, create a record and check that the same number of logs
has been generated. And then delete it, check that it has created log
with x fields updated as per rule

methods.name: odoo.addons.auditlog.tests.test_auditlog.AuditlogCommon.test_LogDuringUpdate
documentation:
Seventh test, check that no exception comes when we log data
and the auditlog rule model is not up to date

classes.name: odoo.addons.auditlog.models.rule.DictDiffer
documentation:
Calculate the difference between two dictionaries as:
(1) items added
(2) items removed
(3) keys same in both but changed values
(4) keys same in both and unchanged values

methods.name: odoo.addons.auditlog.models.rule.AuditlogRule._register_hook
documentation: Get all rules and apply them to log method calls.

methods.name: odoo.addons.auditlog.models.rule.AuditlogRule._patch_methods
documentation: Patch ORM methods of models defined in rules to log their calls.

methods.name: odoo.addons.auditlog.models.rule.AuditlogRule._revert_methods
documentation: Restore original ORM methods of models defined in rules.

methods.name: odoo.addons.auditlog.models.rule.AuditlogRule.create
documentation: Update the registry when a new rule is created.

methods.name: odoo.addons.auditlog.models.rule.AuditlogRule.write
documentation: Update the registry when existing rules are updated.

methods.name: odoo.addons.auditlog.models.rule.AuditlogRule.unlink
documentation: Unsubscribe rules before removing them.

methods.name: odoo.addons.auditlog.models.rule.AuditlogRule.get_auditlog_fields
documentation:
Get the list of auditlog fields for a model
By default it is all stored fields only, but you can
override this.

methods.name: odoo.addons.auditlog.models.rule.AuditlogRule._make_create
documentation: Instanciate a create method that log its calls.

documentation: Instanciate an unlink method that log its calls.

methods.name: odoo.addons.auditlog.models.rule.AuditlogRule.create_logs

Create logs. `old_values` and `new_values` are dictionaries, e.g:

methods.name: odoo.addons.auditlog.models.rule.AuditlogRule.subscribe
documentation:
Subscribe Rule for auditing changes on model and apply shortcut
to view logs on that model.

documentation: Unsubscribe Auditing Rule on model.
